New version available: v0.81.0 (installed v0.80.10). Run: `npm i -g @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent`

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: 唯讀第二意見審查（不改檔）

availableCommands.name: skill:ask-matt
availableCommands.description: Ask which skill or flow fits your situation. A router over the skills in this repo.

availableCommands.name: skill:code-review
availableCommands.description: Review the changes since a fixed point (commit, branch, tag, or merge-base) along two axes — Standards (does the code follow this repo's documented coding standards?) and Spec (does the code match what the originating issue/PRD asked for?). Runs both reviews in parallel sub-agents and reports them side by side. Use when the user wants to review a branch, a PR, work-in-progress changes, or asks to "review since X".

availableCommands.name: skill:codebase-design
availableCommands.description: Shared vocabulary for designing deep modules. Use when the user wants to design or improve a module's interface, find deepening opportunities, decide where a seam goes, make code more testable or AI-navigable, or when another skill needs the deep-module vocabulary.

availableCommands.name: skill:diagnosing-bugs
availableCommands.description: Diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Use when the user says "diagnose"/"debug this", or reports something broken/throwing/failing/slow.

availableCommands.name: skill:domain-modeling
availableCommands.description: Build and sharpen a project's domain model. Use when the user wants to pin down domain terminology or a ubiquitous language, record an architectural decision, or when another skill needs to maintain the domain model.

availableCommands.name: skill:find-skills
availableCommands.description: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.

availableCommands.name: skill:grill-me
availableCommands.description: A relentless interview to sharpen a plan or design.

availableCommands.name: skill:grill-with-docs
availableCommands.description: A relentless interview to sharpen a plan or design, which also creates docs (ADR's and glossary) as we go.

availableCommands.name: skill:grilling
availableCommands.description: Grill the user relentlessly about a plan, decision, or idea. Use when the user wants to stress-test their thinking, or uses any 'grill' trigger phrases.

availableCommands.name: skill:handoff
availableCommands.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.

availableCommands.name: skill:implement
availableCommands.description: Implement a piece of work based on a spec or set of tickets.

availableCommands.name: skill:improve-codebase-architecture
availableCommands.description: Scan a codebase for deepening opportunities, present them as a visual HTML report, then grill through whichever one you pick.

availableCommands.name: skill:n8n-code-javascript
availableCommands.description: Write JavaScript code in n8n Code nodes. Use when writing JavaScript in n8n, using $input/$json/$node syntax, making HTTP requests with this.helpers / the $helpers global, working with dates using DateTime, troubleshooting Code node errors, choosing between Code node modes, or doing any custom data transformation in n8n. Always use this skill when a workflow needs a Code node — whether for data aggregation, filtering, API calls, format conversion, batch processing logic, or any custom JavaScript. Covers SplitInBatches loop patterns, cross-iteration data, pairedItem, and real-world production patterns. Also use when asked why a Code node or workflow is slow, which execution mode is faster, or how to cut per-item overhead on large datasets. EXCEPTION — for the AI-agent-callable Custom Code Tool (@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolCode, a tool attached to an AI Agent), use the n8n-code-tool skill instead; it has a different runtime contract.

availableCommands.name: skill:n8n-expression-syntax
availableCommands.description: Validate n8n expression syntax and fix common errors. Use when writing n8n expressions, using {{}} syntax, accessing $json/$node variables, troubleshooting expression errors, mapping data between nodes, or referencing webhook data in workflows. Use this skill whenever configuring node fields that reference data from previous nodes — expressions are how n8n passes data between nodes, and getting the syntax wrong is the most common source of workflow errors. Also use when asked whether a complex expression hurts performance.

availableCommands.name: skill:n8n-mcp-tools-expert
availableCommands.description: Expert guide for using n8n-mcp MCP tools effectively. Use when searching for nodes, validating configurations, accessing templates, managing workflows, managing credentials, auditing instance security, or using any n8n-mcp tool. Provides tool selection guidance, parameter formats, and common patterns. IMPORTANT — Always consult this skill before calling any n8n-mcp tool — it prevents common mistakes like wrong nodeType formats, incorrect parameter structures, and inefficient tool usage. If the user mentions n8n, workflows, nodes, or automation and you have n8n MCP tools available, use this skill first.

availableCommands.name: skill:n8n-validation-expert
availableCommands.description: Interpret validation errors and guide fixing them. Use when encountering validation errors, validation warnings, false positives, operator structure issues, or need help understanding validation results. Also use when asking about validation profiles, error types, the validation loop process, or auto-fix capabilities. Consult this skill whenever a validate_node or validate_workflow call returns errors or warnings — it knows which warnings are false positives and which errors need real fixes.

availableCommands.name: skill:pi-docs-latest
availableCommands.description: Use this skill whenever the user asks about Pi / pi.dev documentation, Pi packages, extensions, themes, custom providers, custom models, prompt templates, installing or packaging Pi resources, or maintaining Pi-related skills/extensions. On the first invocation each local day, refresh the cached docs from pi.dev and run a self-maintenance pass so this skill stays current.

availableCommands.name: skill:prototype
availableCommands.description: Build a throwaway prototype to answer a design question. Use when the user wants to sanity-check whether a state model or logic feels right, or explore what a UI should look like.

availableCommands.name: skill:research
availableCommands.description: Investigate a question against high-trust primary sources and capture the findings as a Markdown file in the repo. Use when the user wants a topic researched, docs or API facts gathered, or reading legwork delegated to a background agent.

availableCommands.name: skill:security-audit
availableCommands.description: Security audit of a codebase — web apps, APIs, services, CLI tools, libraries, daemons, and more. Use when asked to find security bugs, do a security review, audit for vulnerabilities, or pen-test the code. Focuses on exploitable issues with real impact, not theoretical concerns or industry-standard behavior.

availableCommands.name: skill:setup-matt-pocock-skills
availableCommands.description: Configure this repo for the engineering skills — set up its issue tracker, triage label vocabulary, and domain doc layout. Run once before first use of the other engineering skills.

availableCommands.name: skill:stitch::react-components
availableCommands.description: Converts Stitch designs into modular Vite and React components, or syncs/updates existing React components to align with the latest Stitch designs, using system-level networking and AST-based validation.

availableCommands.name: skill:tdd
availableCommands.description: Test-driven development. Use when the user wants to build features or fix bugs test-first, mentions "red-green-refactor", or wants integration tests.

availableCommands.name: skill:teach
availableCommands.description: Teach the user a new skill or concept, within this workspace.

availableCommands.name: skill:to-spec
availableCommands.description: Turn the current conversation into a spec and publish it to the project issue tracker — no interview, just synthesis of what you've already discussed.

availableCommands.name: skill:to-tickets
availableCommands.description: Break a plan, spec, or the current conversation into a set of tracer-bullet tickets, each declaring its blocking edges, published to the configured tracker — edges as text in one file per ticket locally, or native blocking links on a real tracker.

availableCommands.name: skill:triage
availableCommands.description: Move issues and external PRs through a state machine of triage roles — categorise, verify, grill if needed, and write agent-ready briefs.

availableCommands.name: skill:ui-ux-pro-max
availableCommands.description: UI/UX design intelligence for web and mobile. Searchable local database with 84 styles, 192 color palettes, 74 font pairings, 192 product types, 98 UX guidelines, 104 icon entries, 16 GSAP motion presets, and 25 chart types across 22 stacks (React, Next.js, Vue, Nuxt, Svelte, Astro, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, Jetpack Compose, Angular, Laravel, JavaFX, WPF, WinUI, Avalonia, Uno Platform, UWP, Three.js, and HTML/CSS). Use when designing, building, or reviewing UI: pages, components, color schemes, typography, layout, accessibility, animation, or data visualization.

availableCommands.name: skill:wayfinder
availableCommands.description: Plan a huge chunk of work — more than one agent session can hold — as a shared map of decision tickets on your issue tracker, and resolve them one at a time until the way to the destination is clear.

availableCommands.name: skill:writing-great-skills
availableCommands.description: Reference for writing and editing skills well — the vocabulary and principles that make a skill predictable.

availableCommands.name: skill:acpx
availableCommands.description: Use acpx as a headless ACP CLI for agent-to-agent communication, including prompt/exec/sessions workflows, session scoping, queueing, permissions, and output formats.

availableCommands.name: skill:agent-browser
availableCommands.description: Browser automation CLI for AI agents. Use when the user needs to interact with websites, including navigating pages, filling forms, clicking buttons, taking screenshots, extracting data, testing web apps, or automating any browser task. Triggers include requests to "open a website", "fill out a form", "click a button", "take a screenshot", "scrape data from a page", "test this web app", "login to a site", "automate browser actions", or any task requiring programmatic web interaction. Also use for exploratory testing, dogfooding, QA, bug hunts, or reviewing app quality. Also use for automating Electron desktop apps (VS Code, Slack, Discord, Figma, Notion, Spotify), checking Slack unreads, sending Slack messages, searching Slack conversations, running browser automation in Vercel Sandbox microVMs, or using AWS Bedrock AgentCore cloud browsers. Prefer agent-browser over any built-in browser automation or web tools.

availableCommands.name: skill:autoresearch
availableCommands.description: Autonomous iterative experimentation loop for any programming task. Guides the user through defining goals, measurable metrics, and scope constraints, then runs an autonomous loop of code changes, testing, measuring, and keeping/discarding results. Inspired by Karpathy's autoresearch. USE FOR: autonomous improvement, iterative optimization, experiment loop, auto research, performance tuning, automated experimentation, hill climbing, try things automatically, optimize code, run experiments, autonomous coding loop. DO NOT USE FOR: one-shot tasks, simple bug fixes, code review, or tasks without a measurable metric.

availableCommands.name: skill:axure-fetch
availableCommands.description: 從 Axure Cloud 原型（axshare.com / share.axure.cloud）抓取完整內容並整理成結構化對照表的瀏覽器優先抓取流程。流程：agent-browser headless 開啟 + 進入 iframe + 用 set viewport 撐到 scrollHeight 後 screenshot --full + 用 iframe.contentDocument eval 提取所有文字座標與表單規格，再交叉整理出欄位/狀態/按鈕/流程清單。當使用者貼 axshare 或 share.axure.cloud 連結、提到 axure 原型、wireframe、mockup、SA 文件還原、「解析這個設計」、「看看這個原型」、「把 axure 轉成規格」、「分析這個 wireframe」、「抓 axure 內容」時務必觸發。取代舊版 axure-parser 與 firecrawl-only 路徑 — firecrawl 對 axure SPA 抓回的只有外殼，純腳本又抓不到動態狀態。即使使用者沒明確說 axure，只要 URL 是 axshare.com 或 share.axure.cloud 就應該觸發。

availableCommands.name: skill:design-md
availableCommands.description: Analyze Stitch projects and synthesize a semantic design system into DESIGN.md files

availableCommands.name: skill:design-taste-frontend
availableCommands.description: Anti-slop frontend skill for landing pages, portfolios, and redesigns. The agent reads the brief, infers the right design direction, and ships interfaces that do not look templated. Real design systems when applicable, audit-first on redesigns, strict pre-flight check.

availableCommands.name: skill:develop-web-game
availableCommands.description: Use when Codex is building or iterating on a web game (HTML/JS) and needs a reliable development + testing loop: implement small changes, run a Playwright-based test script with short input bursts and intentional pauses, inspect screenshots/text, and review console errors with render_game_to_text.

availableCommands.name: skill:doc
availableCommands.description: Use when the task involves reading, creating, or editing `.docx` documents, especially when formatting or layout fidelity matters; prefer `python-docx` plus the bundled `scripts/render_docx.py` for visual checks.

availableCommands.name: skill:elysiajs
availableCommands.description: Create backend with ElysiaJS, a type-safe, high-performance framework.

availableCommands.name: skill:firecrawl
availableCommands.description: Search, scrape, and interact with the web via the Firecrawl CLI. Use this skill whenever the user wants to search the web, find articles, research a topic, look something up online, scrape a webpage, grab content from a URL, get data from a website, crawl documentation, download a site, or interact with pages that need clicks or logins. Also use when they say "fetch this page", "pull the content from", "get the page at https://", or reference external websites. This provides real-time web search with full page content and interact capabilities — beyond what Claude can do natively with built-in tools. Do NOT trigger for local file operations, git commands, deployments, or code editing tasks.

availableCommands.name: skill:get-design
availableCommands.description: Fetch and apply production-grade design systems from 70+ major companies (Stripe, Apple, Vercel, Nike, etc.) using the getdesign CLI. Use this skill whenever the user wants to build UI inspired by a specific brand, needs a design reference for colors/typography/components, mentions a company's design style, asks for a DESIGN.md file, or needs help picking a design direction for their project. Also use when building frontend and the user says things like 'make it look like Stripe' or 'I want a clean dark theme like Linear'.

availableCommands.name: skill:gpt-image-studio
availableCommands.description: Use when working in yuuzu-router with gpt-image-2, /v1/images/generations, /v1/images/edits, Image Studio UI, image prompt presets, image editing prompts, or troubleshooting Codex image output quality.

availableCommands.name: skill:handoff-doc
availableCommands.description: 為 web 系統產出「證據級」完整交接文件（離線 HTML 手冊＋本機 assets）：真實截圖、UI 操作錄影、Remotion 流程動畫、live DB 刷新與 drift 偵測、互動 ER 圖、逐畫面章節走查；支援 --detail 深度模式（子功能級分解、函式級呼叫鏈、欄位級資料對照、元素特寫截圖）。當使用者提到「交接文件」「系統交接」「handoff」「接手文件」「維運文件」「系統文件化」「幫新人寫系統說明」，或要求為既有系統產出含截圖/錄影/動畫/資料庫圖的完整技術文件時，務必使用此 skill — 即使使用者只說「幫這個系統寫一份完整文件」而沒有明講交接。其中的單項技巧也適用於獨立需求：「錄一段系統操作演示影片」「幫資料庫畫互動關係圖」「對系統頁面批次截圖」「把某功能分析到每個按鈕跟函式」時同樣載入本 skill 取對應章節。

availableCommands.name: skill:html-first
availableCommands.description: Generate rich, interactive HTML files instead of Markdown for substantial outputs, using the Claude design reference and built-in evaluation/review affordances. Use when producing plans, specs, reports, code reviews, diagrams, prototypes, slide decks, custom editors, benchmark/eval viewers, or documents over about 50 lines that involve visualization, sharing, navigation, interactivity, charts, scoring, or human feedback. Trigger on writing plans, reviewing PRs, creating reports or research, making diagrams or flowcharts, prototyping UI or animations, building throwaway tools, creating decks, presenting eval results, or when the user says make it visual, interactive, HTML output, open in browser, shareable, dashboard, chart, scorecard, or evaluation. Do not trigger for commit messages, short inline chat replies, config file edits, or code-only output.

availableCommands.name: skill:n8n-code-python
availableCommands.description: Write Python code in n8n Code nodes. Use when writing Python in n8n, using _input/_json/_node syntax, working with standard library, or need to understand Python limitations in n8n Code nodes. Use this skill when the user specifically requests Python for an n8n Code node. Note — JavaScript is recommended for 95% of use cases — only use Python when the user explicitly prefers it or the task requires Python-specific standard library capabilities (regex, hashlib, statistics). EXCEPTION — for Python in the AI-agent-callable Custom Code Tool (@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolCode), use the n8n-code-tool skill instead (input is _query, return must be a string).

availableCommands.name: skill:n8n-node-configuration
availableCommands.description: Operation-aware node configuration guidance. Use when configuring nodes, understanding property dependencies, determining required fields, choosing between get_node detail levels, or learning common configuration patterns by node type. Always use this skill when setting up node parameters — it explains which fields are required for each operation, how displayOptions control field visibility, and when to use patchNodeField for surgical edits vs full node updates.

availableCommands.name: skill:n8n-workflow-patterns
availableCommands.description: Proven workflow architectural patterns from real n8n workflows. Use when building new workflows, designing workflow structure, choosing workflow patterns, planning workflow architecture, or asking about webhook processing, HTTP API integration, database operations, AI agent workflows, batch processing, or scheduled tasks. Always consult this skill when the user asks to create, build, or design an n8n workflow, automate a process, or connect services — even if they don't explicitly mention 'patterns'. Covers webhook, API, database, AI, batch processing, and scheduled automation architectures. Also use when optimizing a slow workflow or speeding up large-item-count processing (node count, batchSize, all-items vs per-item).

availableCommands.name: skill:pdf
availableCommands.description: Use when tasks involve reading, creating, or reviewing PDF files where rendering and layout matter; prefer visual checks by rendering pages (Poppler) and use Python tools such as `reportlab`, `pdfplumber`, and `pypdf` for generation and extraction.

availableCommands.name: skill:pi-rescue
availableCommands.description: 交給 pi 實際動手修（不限制工具，可讀寫檔案、執行 bash）。可用 --provider / --model / --effort 客製化，預設 geosense-router gpt-5.6-sol max。當使用者說 pi rescue、交給 pi 修、讓 pi 動手、pi 來救時使用。

availableCommands.name: skill:pi-review
availableCommands.description: 用 pi 對指定範圍做唯讀第二意見審查（不改檔）。可用 --provider / --model / --effort 客製化，預設 geosense-router gpt-5.6-sol max。當使用者說 pi review、pi 審查、找 pi 看一下、要第二意見、second opinion 時使用。

availableCommands.name: skill:project-primer
availableCommands.description: Analyze an unfamiliar repository to identify project structure, tech stack, entry points, tooling, configuration, and documentation, then produce a concise project-understanding report. availableCommands.name: skill:recap-plan-docs
availableCommands.description: Write session handoff documents into `docs/recap` or `docs/codex-plan` as self-contained HTML by default (with markdown fallback), using timestamped filenames in the form `{title}-{YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM-SS}.html`. Use when Codex needs to save a recap for the next session, archive a development plan, create a session handoff note, or persist working context in a repo-local file for future continuation. Output is HTML-first — easier to skim with collapsible sections, TL;DR card, TOC, and print-friendly styling — but raw markdown (`--format md`) is still available when plain text is required.

availableCommands.name: skill:redesign-existing-projects
availableCommands.description: Upgrades existing websites and apps to premium quality. Audits current design, identifies generic AI patterns, and applies high-end design standards without breaking functionality. Works with any CSS framework or vanilla CSS.

availableCommands.name: skill:remotion-best-practices
availableCommands.description: Best practices for Remotion

availableCommands.name: skill:report

availableCommands.name: skill:report-review

availableCommands.name: skill:resolving-merge-conflicts

availableCommands.name: skill:screenshot

availableCommands.name: skill:shadcn

availableCommands.name: skill:skill-creator
availableCommands.description: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. availableCommands.name: skill:spark

availableCommands.name: skill:speech
availableCommands.description: Use when the user asks for text-to-speech narration or voiceover, accessibility reads, audio prompts, or batch speech generation via the OpenAI Audio API; run the bundled CLI (`scripts/text_to_speech.py`) with built-in voices and require `OPENAI_API_KEY` for live calls. Custom voice creation is out of scope.

availableCommands.name: skill:spreadsheet
availableCommands.description: Use when tasks involve creating, editing, analyzing, or formatting spreadsheets (`.xlsx`, `.csv`, `.tsv`) using Python (`openpyxl`, `pandas`), especially when formulas, references, and formatting need to be preserved and verified.

availableCommands.name: skill:sql-cli

availableCommands.name: skill:taste-design
availableCommands.description: Semantic Design System Skill for Google Stitch. Generates agent-friendly DESIGN.md files that enforce premium, anti-generic UI standards — strict typography, calibrated color, asymmetric layouts, perpetual micro-motion, and hardware-accelerated performance.

availableCommands.name: skill:vercel-react-best-practices

This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. availableCommands.name: skill:vercel-react-view-transitions
availableCommands.description: Guide for implementing smooth, native-feeling animations using React's View Transition API (`<ViewTransition>` component, `addTransitionType`, and CSS view transition pseudo-elements). Use this skill whenever the user wants to add page transitions, animate route changes, create shared element animations, animate enter/exit of components, animate list reorder, implement directional (forward/back) navigation animations, or integrate view transitions in Next.js. Also use when the user mentions view transitions, `startViewTransition`, `ViewTransition`, transition types, or asks about animating between UI states in React without third-party animation libraries.

availableCommands.name: skill:vueuse-functions
availableCommands.description: Apply VueUse composables where appropriate to build concise, maintainable Vue.js / Nuxt features.

availableCommands.name: skill:web-design-guidelines
availableCommands.description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".

availableCommands.name: skill:working-daily
availableCommands.description: 自動彙整今日工作並填入 EIP 工作日誌。當使用者提到日誌相關需求時，直接在對話中蒐集 Git commits、會議紀錄和 EIP 專案列表，分析後產生摘要表格（含子項目），與使用者互動確認，確認後用 CLI 填入 EIP。觸發詞：工作日誌、填日誌、fill diary、今天做了什麼、daily log、EIP 日誌、寫日誌、補日誌、日誌自動化、working daily、收工、刪除日誌、刪日誌。也適用於查詢會議紀錄、列出 EIP 專案、查詢子項目、刪除日誌條目、檢查未填日誌、或批次補填多天日誌。即使使用者只說「日誌」或「收工」，都應觸發此 skill。

availableCommands.name: skill:yuuzu-rule
availableCommands.description: Activate Yuuzu's session safety rules—do not edit or delete files, do not run git add/commit or use Git worktrees, and ask which model to use before launching any subagent. Use only when the user explicitly invokes `/skill:yuuzu-rule`, `$yuuzu-rule`, or asks to activate `yuuzu-rule`.
